title: A Land in Peril - Let's Make a Quest (solo RPG) (Design Diary)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_r9bm8JLsKc

A design diary from the workbench: Daniel (@dungeondive) shares progress on A Land in Peril, his own solo game of questing and adventure, with the core quest system now complete. He reads the flavour text — a slumbering Adversary, a bulwark of stoic heroes born to stand against it — and lays out seven design pillars, including simple rules, episodic play that starts at the threshold of adventure, fast combat with no hit points, fail-forward questing, and a roguelite legacy system where quest outcomes reshape the world. Then he builds a quest live. Warrior Kregor (fast piercing rapier, magic keyword) draws an urban castle setting, a destroy objective, a mysterious note for a source, and nobles plus a weather-controlling weapon for ingredients — assembling into a mission to infiltrate a castle and destroy a corrupt wizard's storm-summoning device, garnished with the ominous flavour "many people have tried and failed." The milestone-tracking system gets a workout too: minor challenges accumulate until a quest die promotes one to a major milestone, with determination spent to retry failures and a fate track that follows the hero between quests toward the Hall of Heroes or a legend's death. Target: written by December 2024, released 2025, with the oracle spinning off to itch.io first.

----

One of Daniel's design pillars is abstracting everything between quests — if it matters, it becomes a quest of its own. Do you prefer that episodic cut-to-the-action structure, or do the quiet travelling stretches matter to your solo play?
